verdict_summary: V7-F envelope reached browser (HTTP 200, 1207-byte text/event-stream, single event: message with 4 termination markers) but DevTools EventStream parsed 0 events and React answer-block did not mount; in addition, HAR body shows Korean warning text is mojibake (UTF-8 bytes appear as CP949/EUC-KR-style garbled characters like '창큄혻챦쨍혧'), so even if SPA had parsed it the user would not have seen '차단'.

evidence.console_errors_excerpt: Access to script at 'https://perplexity-ai.cloudflareaccess.com/cdn-cgi/access/login/...restricted-feature-loader-DrbzplZ3.js' (redirected from 'https://pplx-next-static-public.perplexity.ai/_spa/assets/_restricted/restricted-feature-loader-DrbzplZ3.js') from origin 'https://www.perplexity.ai' has been blocked by CORS policy: No 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' header is present on the requested resource. | GET .../restricted-feature-loader-DrbzplZ3.js net::ERR_FAILED 200 (OK) | FedCM was disabled either temporarily based on previous user action | [GSI_LOGGER]: FedCM get() rejects with NetworkError: Error retrieving a token.

recommendation: INVESTIGATE_TWO_FAILURES_V7G — (1) Mojibake: Korean warning text in 'chunks' and 'answer' fields is CORRUPTED (e.g. '창큄혻챦쨍혧' = UTF-8 bytes interpreted as CP949). The C++ envelope generator is likely emitting the Korean string with the wrong encoding or double-encoding UTF-8 → CP949. Inspect template loader / source file encoding for #750 baseline match. (2) DevTools EventStream parsed 0 events even though body IS in HAR with status 200 + correct content-type — this suggests the SSE stream framing is still off (possibly missing trailing blank line per spec '\n\n', missing initial chunk flush, or H2 END_STREAM timing prevents Chrome's SSE parser from emitting the event before stream close). Compare byte-level with deepseek's working SSE frames. Also note Cloudflare Access CORS errors on restricted-feature-loader continue but do not affect the main answer pipeline (those are auxiliary scripts).
notes: Anonymous session (logged_in=false, '로그인' button visible). Cloudflare Access CORS errors persist on restricted-feature-loader-*.js (redirected to perplexity-ai.cloudflareaccess.com) but main intercept fired correctly. The fact that EventStream count = 0 while HAR body = full 1207 bytes is strong evidence that the SSE stream is not being framed correctly for Chrome's incremental EventSource parser; the body is only captured by HAR after final close. HAR saved to gitignored files/769/.
